findings.title: oneOf variant breaks MissingRequiredArtifactError and resolveRequirement
findings.detail: The design adds a `{ domain: "change"; oneOf: readonly SingletonChangeArtifactType[] }` variant to `ArtifactRequirement`. However, `MissingRequiredArtifactError` (line 206-222) accesses `requirement.type` which does not exist on the `oneOf` variant, and `resolveRequirement()` (line 85-108) switches on `requirement.type`/`requirement.domain` without handling the `oneOf` case. Both functions need to be updated to handle the new variant. Tasks 7.1–7.2 should explicitly include updating `MissingRequiredArtifactError` to format the `oneOf` variant correctly, and updating `resolveRequirement()` to resolve `oneOf` requirements by checking each type in order.

findings.title: No task for design skill integration
findings.detail: The proposal lists `/specflow.design` skill as an impact area: 'tasks generation flow switches from OpenSpec passthrough to specflow task-planner invocation.' The spec also states 'New changes always generate task graph' at the end of the design phase. However, the tasks have no step for updating the design skill/command to call `generateTaskGraph()` + `renderTasksMd()` instead of the OpenSpec tasks template. Without this integration task, the task-planner module will be built but never invoked. Add a task (after bundle 8) to wire the design phase command/skill to call the new task-planner pipeline and disable the OpenSpec tasks passthrough.

findings.title: No task for apply phase write-back integration
findings.detail: The spec requires the apply phase to read task-graph for next window selection and write back status (`pending → in_progress → done`). Tasks 5–6 implement the pure functions (`selectNextWindow`, `updateBundleStatus`), but no task wires these into the actual apply phase runtime (e.g., `src/core/run-commands.ts` or the apply skill). Add a task to integrate window selection and status write-back into the apply phase, including the legacy fallback path.

findings.title: Task 7.1 places oneOf in wrong file
findings.detail: Task 7.1 says 'Add `oneOf` variant to `ArtifactRequirement` type in `src/lib/artifact-types.ts`'. This is correct for the type definition. But it should also note that a `oneOf()` helper function (analogous to the existing `req()` function in `artifact-phase-gates.ts` line 27-31) needs to be created. The `req()` helper is defined in the gates file, not in artifact-types.ts, so the helper should go in the same file. Consider clarifying this in task 7.1 or 7.2.
